description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a striking blue fuselage and wings, accented by white tail surfaces and black cross insignias reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The airplane features two sets of straight, parallel wings connected by a network of black struts and wires, with the upper wing slightly larger than the lower. The nose is equipped with a wooden two-blade propeller and a pair of exposed, cylindrical engine housings mounted between the wings. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windscreen and visible pilot seat. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted white with black crosses. The overall construction appears to be primarily metal and wood, with fabric-covered wings and control surfaces.
description: A vintage blue biplane with dual wings, exposed engines, and an open cockpit, featuring black cross insignias and a wooden propeller. The design is reminiscent of early military aircraft with a mix of metal, wood, and fabric construction.
description_view_0: Rear three-quarter view showing the blue fuselage, white tail with black cross, and the upper and lower wings with visible struts and wires.
description_view_1: Direct top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, dual engine housings, and the black cross insignias on both wings and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite rear three-quarter view, similar to view 0 but from the other side, showing the tail, wing structure, and engine placement.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the wooden propeller, engine housings, open cockpit, and the symmetrical biplane wing arrangement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and connected by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin exposed piston engines mounted between the upper and lower wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; all tail surfaces are straight-edged.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a small tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located forward of the fuselage, just behind the upper wing, with a small windscreen.
